_spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: All the pure logic in search_boards is now extracted and tested: parse, filter, formatting, and (this loop) the per-board render. search_boards is down to its orchestration role. The remaining ceiling is that the orchestration itself (pagination, cross-page dedup, early-stop, near-miss suggestions) and the SoundboardSnag download pipeline are network-coupled and untested because there is no injectable fetch seam.

architecture_quality.proof: pure parse/filter/format extracted; the per-board render extraction is this loop's fix (scored next loop). Residual: no fetch seam, so the orchestration loop and SoundboardSnag pipeline are untestable

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; immutable instance attrs

domain_modeling.proof: BoardResult + ParsedBoard; sounds_info deliberately a plain (id,title) 2-tuple (guardrail: don't force types)

data_flow.proof: named-field contracts; residual: main re-filters results (soundboard-snag.py:1593) although search_boards already returns only downloadable boards (1299) — a redundant filter (F-007)

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; defensive sanitization; HTTPS; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: render duplication removed via two pure helpers (commit 41ec0c7); search_boards shrank to ~636. Residual at loop start: the per-board detail render still inlined (addressed this loop)

test_strategy.proof: FormatUpdatedLineTests + FormatSkippedBreakdownTests added (commit 41ec0c7); 39 tests cover parse, filter and formatting at real Interfaces. Residual: network orchestration + download pipeline untested (F-008)

credibility.proof: parse, filter and formatting all test-backed; consistent behavior-preserving extraction history; code remains honest

strengths: Five behavior-preserving, independently-reviewed extractions (BoardResult, _parse_board_html, _evaluate_filters, two format helpers, render helper) — every slice tested at its Interface
search_boards is now an orchestration function; all of its pure logic is reachable and tested without the network
Synchronous design + defensive sanitization remain real strengths

findings.title: search_boards still inlines the per-board detail render
findings.why_it_matters: The detail render is sequential formatting that should be a tested helper, keeping search_boards larger than its orchestration role and the output untested.
findings.what_is_wrong: The final results listing inlined the per-board detail render (name/status, URL, description, category, views, dates, tags, sample files).
findings.leverage_impact: Maintainers read the detail render inside the orchestration function; output is untested.
findings.locality_impact: A formatting change is localized but was untested.
findings.why_weakens_submission: The last inlined render kept architecture and simplicity a notch below top-tier and the output untested.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract a pure _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str]; the loop prints the lines; fixture-test the lines' semantic content. No renderer class.

findings.title: Network orchestration and the download pipeline are untestable (no injectable fetch seam)
findings.why_it_matters: The genuinely complex untested logic — pagination, cross-page dedup, the early-stop on enough-downloadable, near-miss suggestions, and SoundboardSnag.snag's consecutive-failure abort — can only be exercised against the live site, so it has no regression guard.
findings.what_is_wrong: search_boards and SoundboardSnag call urlopen directly; there is no seam to inject a fake page-fetcher, so the orchestration and download loops cannot be unit-tested.
evidence: soundboard-snag.py:789-1404 (search_boards orchestration: pagination, dedup, early-stop, near-miss)
findings.leverage_impact: Callers/maintainers cannot verify the orchestration without the network.
findings.locality_impact: A regression in pagination or the early-stop would only surface against the live site.
findings.why_weakens_submission: The remaining test-strategy and architecture ceiling: the most behavior-rich code is untested. A fetch seam (real urlopen adapter + in-memory fake) satisfies the Unified Seam Policy two-adapter rule and unlocks deterministic tests.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Introduce a small fetch seam: a module-level _http_get(url) -> str default, threaded as an optional `fetch` parameter into search_boards (and a `fetcher` into SoundboardSnag). Production passes the default; tests pass an in-memory fake returning canned HTML keyed by URL. Then test pagination/dedup/early-stop/near-miss and the consecutive-failure abort. Two real adapters (urlopen + fake) justify the seam.

findings.title: main re-filters search results that are already downloadable-only
findings.why_it_matters: A redundant filter implies search_boards might return play-only boards, which it does not; the duplication is mildly misleading.
findings.what_is_wrong: main computes downloadable_boards = [r for r in results if r.has_downloads], but search_boards already returns only has_downloads boards.
findings.leverage_impact: None — the filter is a no-op.
findings.locality_impact: Minor: a reader may wonder whether search_boards can return play-only boards.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Redundant defensive code is a small data-flow/simplicity deduction.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Replace the re-filter with downloadable_boards = results (or iterate results directly), with a comment that search_boards already guarantees has_downloads.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Extracting _render_board_lines passes the Shallow-module test: a small Interface (board + stats + flag in, list[str] out) with the detail-render behavior behind it, now reached directly by tests. The render is pure (depends only on the BoardResult and module constants).
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: search_boards drops to ~615 lines and the results listing is now testable.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not move ANSI out of the render helper (the colors are intrinsic to each line); do not extract the progress/diagnostic prints; no renderer class. The F-008 fetch seam is a separate, larger change for a later loop.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: test_soundboard_snag.py gains RenderBoardLinesTests (header/status, play-only, optional fields, dates-only-when-flag, sample files, empty samples) asserting semantic content with ANSI stripped.

backlog.title: Extract the per-board detail render into a pure tested _render_board_lines (F-006)
backlog.why_it_matters: Final render slice; makes the listing testable and shrinks search_boards to orchestration.

backlog.title: Introduce an injectable fetch seam to test the orchestration and download pipeline (F-008)
backlog.why_it_matters: The remaining test-strategy/architecture ceiling; unlocks deterministic tests of pagination, dedup, early-stop, near-miss, and the failure-abort.

backlog.title: Remove the redundant downloadable re-filter in main (F-007)
backlog.why_it_matters: Subtractive; clarifies that search_boards already guarantees downloadable-only.

deepening_candidates.candidate_module: Page-fetch seam for search_boards and SoundboardSnag
deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F-008 — the orchestration and download loops cannot be tested without the network
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: urlopen is called inline at multiple sites; there is no injection point for a deterministic fake.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: HTTP GET of a page/track URL returning decoded text (or bytes for downloads), behind a default _http_get and an injectable fetch parameter.
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: test_soundboard_snag.py drives search_boards with an in-memory fetcher returning canned HTML; asserts pagination, dedup, early-stop, near-miss, and the consecutive-failure abort.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Add _http_get(url) -> str and an optional fetch=_http_get parameter on search_boards; route the search-page and board-page reads through it.
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not build an HTTP client class hierarchy or a registry of adapters; a single injected function is the honest seam.

builder_notes.pattern: Render functions return lines; the caller prints
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A block of print() calls builds one logical unit of output inside a bigger loop.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Return a list[str] of formatted lines from a pure function; the loop prints them. Test the lines, not stdout.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str]; tests strip ANSI and assert semantic content.

builder_notes.pattern: Color is intrinsic to a render helper; do not factor it out
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Each line interleaves several colored segments, unlike the earlier single-field format helpers.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Let the render helper own the color; assert on ANSI-stripped output in tests instead of forcing plain-text returns.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: the board line mixes BOLD/CYAN/GREEN/GRAY — keep them in the helper.

builder_notes.pattern: Name the seam that unlocks the last untested surface
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: All pure logic is tested, but the network orchestration still has no regression guard.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Introduce a single injectable fetch function (default real, fake in tests) — two adapters justify the seam under the two-adapter rule.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: search_boards(fetch=_http_get) lets tests pass an in-memory fetcher.

narrative: Place — a good app, now with all of search_boards' pure logic extracted and tested. This loop extracts the per-board render into a fixture-tested helper, finishing the decomposition that began with BoardResult. The honest remaining ceiling is named precisely (F-008): the network orchestration and download pipeline have no injectable fetch seam, so the most behavior-rich code is untested. A single injected fetch function (real + fake) is the justified next move. The redundant re-filter in main (F-007) is a small subtractive cleanup. The refactor has stayed disciplined and test-backed throughout.

loop_result.what_changed: Extracted the per-board detail render out of search_boards into a pure _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str]; the results loop now prints the returned lines. Added RenderBoardLinesTests (6 tests) asserting header/status, play-only, optional-field presence, dates-only-when-flag, sample-file listing, and empty-samples, with ANSI stripped.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 45 tests, all OK (39 prior + 6 new); --help exits 0; a direct render comparison shows the produced lines are byte-identical to the original prints (colors, the '\n'-prefixed Sample-files header, and the {idx:2} numbering preserved); search_boards shrank ~636->615 lines.

implementation_review.reason: The per-board render is fully extracted into a pure module-level _render_board_lines returning list[str]; the loop body is the required one-liner; all format strings, field order, and ANSI codes are byte-identical to the originals; six semantic tests cover the new interface with ANSI stripped; no regression introduced.
